# Code of Conduct

## Our Commitment

Ground Zero Community is dedicated to providing a welcoming and inclusive environment for all members. We are committed to fostering a community where everyone feels respected, valued, and safe.

## Our Standards

Examples of behavior that contributes to creating a positive environment include:

- Being kind and respectful to one another
- Listening actively and being open to different perspectives
- Focusing on learning and growth together
- Being patient and supportive with members at all skill levels
- Accepting constructive feedback gracefully
- Respecting others' time, effort, and contributions
- Celebrating achievements and progress of community members

Examples of unacceptable behavior include:

- Harassment, intimidation, or bullying of any kind
- Discrimination based on race, gender, sexual orientation, disability, or any other characteristic
- Unwelcome sexual advances or attention
- Sharing others' private information without consent
- Trolling, spamming, or disruptive behavior
- Making derogatory comments about skills or knowledge levels
- Creating a hostile or uncomfortable environment

## Enforcement

Community members who violate this code of conduct may face temporary or permanent removal from the community, at the discretion of community organizers.

If you witness or experience behavior that violates this code of conduct, please report it to the community organizers at **info@groundzero.tech**.

## Scope

This code of conduct applies to all community spaces, including:
- GitHub repositories and discussions
- Discord server
- Facebook groups
- Community events (online and in-person)

## Attribution

This code of conduct is adapted from the [Contributor Covenant](https://www.contributor-covenant.org/), version 2.0.

---

Remember: We're all learning and growing together. Be humble, be kind, and be a good steward of this community.
